title: Starter questions for each stage
question: How does the tool help people decide what to log at each stage, without driving them?
rationale: Ship a curated set of starter questions for each lifecycle stage, surfaced through the skill; answering one captures a normal decision. It guides what to decide without a wizard — nothing is written until the user confirms, so the tool stays capture-only.

options.label: Curated starter questions, surfaced in the skill
options.note: A fixed set of ~3–6 common decisions per SDLC phase. Invoked by a dedicated subcommand for a stage; answering one runs normal confirm-at-the-moment capture (a decided decision). Coverage-aware: a starter drops off once that phase has a decision covering it.

tradeoffs.note: turns the log into a 'what to decide next' system, the original ask
questions are prompts; nothing is written until the user answers and confirms
same curated set for everyone, so it can be read and improved
must curate a good question set for each phase

options.label: Show suggested questions in the viewer only
options.note: Empty phases display their starter questions as greyed, un-actionable reminders.

tradeoffs.note: zero capture change — a pure review cue
shows the gap but doesn't help you actually decide

options.label: AI generates the questions per project
options.note: The skill invents starter questions tailored to what you're building, each time.

tradeoffs.note: tailored to your app, but less of a shared system
different every run, nothing fixed to read or refine
still only prompts until you confirm
